_meta.purpose: Addendum covering April 18 work: integration of ChatGPT's 3 methodology briefings, HIVIP naming promotion, nuclear staircase analysis results, and refresh chain v3.1 update. Read the April 17 briefing first, then this.

executive_summary: ChatGPT's 3 methodology briefings (Precedent/Lineage, Project Arc/Contamination, Nuclear Assessment) received and integrated into the refresh chain. All 4 refresh files updated to v3.1. HIVIP established as the umbrella methodology name. Nuclear staircase analysis completed: prime-2 mechanism confirmed (structural identity), prime Z enrichment killed (p=0.85), stability corridor predicted (Z=114-126, N≈184). Refresh chain now v3.1.

chatgpt_briefings_integrated.description: All 3 JSONs placed in briefings/ and their content folded into FAST_REFRESH v3.1

files.content_summary: 8-stage precedent chain from Isotropic Principle through HUF/EITT. HIVIP naming architecture. 6-level promotion ladder. 3-path contamination doctrine. 15-item methods status table.
7-stage project arc (EXP-01→prediction). Contamination doctrine formalized with 3 paths. Clean research principles.
Nuclear programme assessment. Corridor prediction. Prime-step staircase mechanics. 3-tier prediction stack. Verification tasks. Forbidden overclaims.

hivip_methodology_established.hierarchy: EITT is the science. The Higgins Decomposition is the engine. HIVP is the chain. HIVIP is the full methodology.

hivp.role: Regression-chain rule: advancing the method without breaking prior DUTs

hivip.full: Higgins Iterative Validation and Integrity Protocol
hivip.role: Umbrella methodology: theory + instrument development + contamination control + regression discipline

promotion_ladder.anti_corruption_rule: No concept climbs by improving one domain at cost of contaminating earlier DUTs

contamination_doctrine.integrity_rule: Prefer weaker but cleaner claim over stronger contaminated one

nuclear_staircase_results.description: Full analysis of AME2020 binding-energy data using EITT compositional decomposition (SEMF 4-part composition on simplex).
scripts: nuclear_staircase_primes.py — step analysis, prime enrichment Monte Carlo, σ²_A extrema
nuclear_stability_islands.py — stability island prediction, SEMF landscape, extrapolation

prime_2_mechanism.finding: Every staircase step is dN = 0, 2, 4, or 6. The prime number 2 IS the mechanism (pairing energy imposes strict parity).
prime_2_mechanism.status: CONFIRMED — structural identity, not statistical correlation

prime_z_enrichment.finding: Zero out of 76 staircase N values are prime. Monte Carlo p=0.85.

stability_corridor.finding: Extrapolation predicts stability corridor Z=114-126, N≈184
stability_corridor.confidence: Moderate for corridor extent, low for exact Z

sigma2_a_candidate.finding: Compositional heat capacity minimum at Z=115, N=252
sigma2_a_candidate.status: Single candidate beyond Z=118, needs verification

packing_crisis.finding: Z≈522 — sustained dN=4 becomes norm, prime 2 loses grip

forbidden_overclaims: Do NOT claim prime numbers predict nuclear stability (p=0.85, dead)
Do NOT claim exact Z for island of stability (corridor-level resolution only)
Do NOT claim EITT discovers new physics (it reads existing SEMF structure through a compositional lens)

what_changed_in_fast_refresh: New methodology section: promotion_ladder, contamination_doctrine, precedent_lineage

verification_tasks_for_chatgpt.instruction: Verify that FAST_REFRESH v3.1 correctly distinguishes HIVP (chain rule) from HIVIP (umbrella methodology). Check that the canonical_names entries are consistent with the methodology section.

Verify that the nuclear_programme section in FAST_REFRESH accurately represents the findings. Prime-2 is structural identity, prime Z is dead, corridor is corridor-level only. Check against your own Nuclear Assessment briefing.
Verify the promotion ladder in FAST_REFRESH matches the 6-level scheme in your Precedent_and_Lineage briefing. Are the level names and descriptions consistent?
Verify the 3-path contamination doctrine in FAST_REFRESH matches your Project_Arc briefing. Is the integrity rule stated correctly?
You were asked to validate the geometric mean proof. Is the claim sound? Is '0.18% vs 20-40%' a fair characterization?
